reviews.review_msg: Patient but prefers to give a nudge in the right direction if asked for help rather than just giving the answer - better for genuine learning. Try to consistently sit with the same group of people and learn to work collaboratively to help each other understand the material during study time; great opportunity to reinforce comprehension by teaching.

There are no lectures. He spends one full class doing practice problems and then the second, he spends 15 minutes on a quiz and then back to problems. Mind you, the problems were not helpful for the midterms or the final. The first midterm was alright but the second one was terrible because he did not prepare us for it. The final was the worst.

Cannot stress how extremely condescending he is towards students. His teaching skills are horrid. He'll write out an entire problem across the board, check and find out what he wrote was wrong, and just move on without teaching the correct way. Laughed at students who didn't understand, and encouraged students to drop the course. Beyond horrible.

Horrible. Horrible. Horrible. He's more interested in showing us how smart he is, in a rather snobby way. Unorganized lectures, terrible at teaching concepts. The textbook saved me, classes were a waste of time except used the iClicker for participation, which was a waste of my money since he didn't really use it as a discussion point. AVOID!

This may be essential if you want to do well in any Calculus courses, but it didn't help me for MATH 157. I did fair in this class, but that's because I worked hard and read the textbook, which explains more clearly than him. Avoid him at all cost. He doesn't even have PhD in Math nor has a background in it. He said it himself.

Try to avoid him, can't believe that he want to control the average grade at C (but he said it),so ridiculous, never realize this is  a 100 level course and for arts students not science. Never teach topics in clear terms, a good prof should know how to teach and motivate students , but he never do. Exams are difficult. we are not major in math !!!

Shouldn't be teaching class. No regard for students, cocky, not wary these are ARTS students forced to take the course + the majority struggle in the topic.  Attending class+all office hours he is little to no help, nor are TAs. Can't explain the topics in clear terms, and assigns hmwrk he doesn't teach. Learned everything on Khan Academy but SVG.

Terrible prof can't explain anything his midterms and tests are incredibly difficult and he's really arrogant and thinks he is really smart even though half the class is failing.  Avoid him at all costs...

Took Intro to Differential Equations with Cameron in Fall 2014. I enjoyed his class because he wasn't one of those profs who reads from the textbook. His examples were good and his assignments tested your understanding. His flaw: he tends to get confused during some of his explanations. Don't bother with the textbook - use Paul's Online Math Notes.
He wears awesome shirts. His lectures are helpful if you do the assignments, but if you go to class without doing the assignments, you won't understand it.
